task_completion.description: Locate and select the target case in the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jurism application is open and the case 'Gideon v. Wainwright' is selected in the central item list.

task_completion.description: Access the 'Significance' note to retrieve its text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A note containing the text 'Overruled Betts v. Brady' is visible in the right-hand pane or note editor, and the text is being highlighted or copied.

task_completion.description: Open the 'Holding' note to prepare for merging.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Holding' note containing the text 'The Sixth Amendment right to counsel' is open in the note editor.

task_completion.description: Merge the significance text into the holding note.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The note editor displays a single note containing BOTH 'The Sixth Amendment right to counsel' AND 'Overruled Betts v. Brady' in the same text area.

task_completion.description: Delete the separate 'Significance' note so only the consolidated note remains.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The item hierarchy under 'Gideon v. Wainwright' in the central pane shows exactly one note attachment remaining, confirming the extra note was deleted.

integrity.description: Agent used the Jurism GUI to edit and delete notes.
integrity.visual_evidence: All note viewing, editing, and deleting actions occur within the Jurism application interface, not via terminal commands.

integrity.description: Agent did not use SQLite commands to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open showing sqlite3 commands or scripts modifying the Jurism database directly.

integrity.description: Agent did not use an external text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text merging occurs entirely within Jurism's built-in note editor, not in an external application like gedit, nano, or a web browser.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Application: Jurism. Target Case: 'Gideon v. Wainwright'. Required Text 1 (from Holding note): 'The Sixth Amendment right to counsel'. Required Text 2 (from Significance note): 'Overruled Betts v. Brady'. Expected Final State: Exactly 1 note attached to the case containing both required texts.
